Ilmarinen - Empires and Puzzles Hero Review & Strategy Guide
Is the master blacksmith of Kalevala still worth the rings? We examine Ilmarinen's base form, his powerful mana cut, and why he's become a niche specialist in a world of speedsters.
Ilmarinen Review: The Hammer That Forged a Niche
Ilmarinen: The Slow-Burning Smith of Kalevala

Ilmarinen, the legendary blacksmith of Finnish mythology, arrived in Empires & Puzzles in June 2022 as part of the Kalevala event. A 5-star Fire hero, he brought a toolkit that screamed "control" with a massive mana cut and a unique enemy damage share mechanic. While his base form has certainly shown its age in the stat department, his specific combination of skills keeps him relevant for players who enjoy watching titans crumble or enemies sharing their pain. He is a Slow hero, which is often a death sentence in modern raids, but don't count the old smith out just yet—he still has a few tricks up his soot-stained sleeves.

Ilmarinen is depicted as a burly, cheerful blacksmith, radiating the warmth of his forge. With a massive hammer in one hand and a crafting tool in the other, he looks ready to build a Sampo or smash a goblin. The art style fits the rustic, mythical vibe of the Kalevala family perfectly. Compared to his "Gold" costume, which leans into a steampunk-industrial aesthetic with goggles and more intricate gear, the original feels more traditional and grounded. He looks like the kind of guy who would buy you a mead after flattening your defense team.

Let's not beat around the bush: Ilmarinen's base stats are from a bygone era. With 756 Attack, 819 Defense, and 1472 Health, he is severely underpowered compared to modern 2025/2026 heroes who regularly flaunt 1300+ Attack and 2200+ Health out of the box. In the current meta, his survivability is questionable without Limit Breaking and heavy embleming. He is a "Fighter" class, which gives him a chance to revive, a crucial survival tool for a slow hero who might die before firing. However, without his costume bonus or modern stats, he is essentially a glass cannon that fires slowly—a dangerous combination.
"Forged in Fire" is a high-risk, high-reward skill.
A 40% mana cut to Ice enemies and 20% to others is significant. In Rush wars, this can completely stall an offense. However, at Slow speed, you often fire this
the enemy has already unleashed their specials, diminishing its value.
This is his signature move. Forcing enemies to share damage (Soul Connect) turns snipers into AoE hitters and AoE hitters into nukes. It is undispellable, which is massive.
-56% defense is a top-tier debuff, rivaling the likes of regular Wilbur but with a higher percentage.
The skill is significantly stronger against Ice enemies, making him a specific counter to Blue-heavy teams, though bringing a Red hero against Blues is always a gamble.
The costume upgrades every single number here. The mana cut jumps to 50%/30%, and the defense down hits -60%. If you have the costume, the base skill feels like a prototype.
Slow (12 tiles). With a lvl 29 Mana troop (+15%) and his family bonus, you can shave a tile or two off, but he will never be "fast."
0 direct damage. His damage contribution is entirely enabling
-56%. On a Titan with 5000 defense, this strips 2800 defense points alone. This is his primary claim to fame.
With base stats of ~1472 HP and ~819 Def, his effective health is vastly lower than modern tanks who sit at ~6000+ effective HP. He relies heavily on his passive heal to survive.
Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities
The Kalevala family offers unique protections against their weaker element, and Ilmarinen's passive is a lifesaver.
Receiving less damage from Special Skills of the stronger element (Ice for Ilmarinen) is excellent. It helps mitigate the risk of bringing him against Blue titans or defenses.

is fantastic. Recovering 15% health and gaining 15% mana when dropping to low health can be the clutch moment that allows him to fire his skill right before dying. For a slow hero, this passive mana gain is often the difference between victory and defeat.

is... okay. Since Ilmarinen doesn't deal direct damage with his special, this only boosts his tile damage. While useful for Titans, a Defensive or Mana-boosting Aether Power would have been far superior for his role.
Upon release, Ilmarinen was hailed as "Wilbur's Big Brother." He was a Titan specialist and a Rush Attack menace. Players loved the massive defense down but lamented the slow speed.
The stat gap is undeniable. Modern 4-star heroes now have better stats than base Ilmarinen. His slow speed makes him a liability in standard raids where speed is king.
He remains a top-tier choice for Green Titans due to the massive -56% Defense Down and high tile damage (especially if Limit Broken).
He is still a nuisance in Rush Attack tournaments and wars, especially if he manages to fire and link the enemy team.
He is simply too slow and fragile for modern diamond arena raids.
The costume is a straight upgrade in almost every regard.
The costume brings his stats into the 2026 era (1151 Attack vs 756 Base).
The Costume increases the Defense Down to -60% and the Mana Cut to -50% for Ice enemies.
The Costume is a Monk (Resist Ailments), while the Base is a Fighter (Revive). This is the
area where the Base might arguably have a niche advantage (the chance to revive is powerful on defense), but the Monk's resistance is generally more consistent.
If you have the costume, use the costume. The stats alone make the base version obsolete for everything except maybe a very specific Fighter class quest.
Since Ilmarinen's main job is to survive long enough to fire his skill (applying DD and Damage Share), durability is key.
Maximizes his low base defense and HP, giving him the best chance to survive tile dumps or sniper hits.
Maximizes tile damage for high scores on Green Titans.
Makes him extremely squishy in PvP.
path (Defense/Health) is recommended to ensure he lives long enough to be useful. However, if you
use him for Titans, go full Attack.
-56% Defense Down is huge for high scores.
Massive mana cut and damage share can end games in Very Fast modes.
His status effects against Ice enemies cannot be cleansed, which is a hard counter to blue cleansers.
The Power of Sisu provides a great survival safety net.
Slow mana is a major liability in the current fast-paced meta.
Base stats are very low, making him fragile without heavy investment.
He relies entirely on teammates to capitalize on his debuffs.
His full potential is locked behind facing Ice enemies, which is risky for a Red hero.
His best role. High DD and tile damage.
Where his speed penalty is negated.
Useful for achieving high scores by linking enemies and applying DD, allowing for massive AoE wipes.
Ilmarinen is a "setup" hero. He needs a "payoff" hero to follow him.
Miki (Atk Buff) - Guardian Falcon (EDD) - Ilmarinen (DD) - Phenexa (Crit/Reset) - Khufu (Hit All)
This stack maximizes damage. Ilmarinen provides the general defense down that stacks with Falcon's elemental down.
Ludwig protects Ilmarinen. Once Ilmarinen fires, the enemies share damage. Then Khufu and Colen hit everyone, dealing compounded damage due to the link.
Can resist his mana cut or defense down.
Prevent him from affecting the whole team.
Hansel, Gretel, or Proteus can stop him from ever firing.
Their skills overwrite each other. Do not bring both.

If you pulled Ilmarinen early, he is a game-changer for your Titan scores and map progress. His ability to make enemies share damage helps clear tough boss waves. Level him, but be aware that he will struggle in raids as you climb higher.

The base version of Ilmarinen is largely a relic. If you don't have the costume, he is likely a bench-warmer outside of Rush wars or a backup Titan team. Do not waste Alpha Aethers on the base version unless you are chasing top 100 event scores and lack better options.
The community has always had a love-hate relationship with Ilmarinen.
“So a 5-star version of Wilbur who also mana cuts? Yes please.”
“He’s so bad, even if they changed his mana speed to average he’d barely make it as a four-star hero.”
— A disgruntled summoner comparing him unfavorably to Li Xiu.
“I put him on tank in this Rush war and so far he’s holding the team nicely... he punishes blues greatly.”
— A defender of his Rush War utility.

Base Ilmarinen is like a classic muscle car: cool to look at, fun to take out on Sundays (Rush Wars), but it lacks the safety features and performance of a modern vehicle (Costume/New Heroes). He defined a niche for Titan damage and event scoring, but without his costume, he is slowly fading into history. If you have him, cherish him for his Titan hits, but pray for his costume.
A retired general, occasionally called back for special operations (Titans).
A comprehensive review of Ilmarinen, the 5-star Fire hero from the Kalevala family. We analyze his stats, special skill 'Forged in Fire', and viability in the modern Empires & Puzzles meta.
